mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeflow
mission.description: Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit designed for Kubernetes, providing a comprehensive platform for building, deploying, and managing machine learning workflows. It is particularly useful for AI practitioners and platform administrators looking for modular and scalable solutions. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for Kubernetes expertise.

steps.title: Install the Kubeflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kubeflow command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing your Kubeflow deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kubeflow/kubeflow/releases/download/v1.10.0/kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf kubeflow-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv kubeflow /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest Kubeflow CLI.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Google Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kubeflow-cluster --num-nodes=3 --cluster-version=1.30 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status.

steps.title: Deploy Kubeflow
steps.description:
This step deploys Kubeflow to your Kubernetes cluster using the CLI.

```bash

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

```

> Deploy Kubeflow using the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures essential components such as autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/monitoring/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubeflow/manifests/v1.10.0/autoscaling/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Set up monitoring for your Kubeflow deployment.
> Enable autoscaling for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kubeflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes Kubeflow from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

kfctl delete -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

gcloud container clusters delete kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Delete the Kubeflow deployment.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubeflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade your Kubeflow installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kubeflow > kubeflow-backup.yaml

kfctl apply -V -f https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/archive/v1.10.0.tar.gz

kubectl get pods -n kubeflow

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Kubeflow.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl CLI is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the installation instructions from the Kubernetes documentation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kubeflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Check node resource availability and consider adding more nodes or increasing node size.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubeflow namespace has not been created or the deployment failed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that Kubeflow was deployed successfully and check the status with 'kubectl get namespaces'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new ML frameworks, improved integration with Kubernetes 1.30, and enhanced UI features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.9, ensure to migrate your configurations and backup your data.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kubeflow' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
